task_groups.title: Implement configuration loading and CLI flag parsing

subtasks.title: Define Config struct with all documented fields and TOML tags
details: Create internal/config/config.go
Define Config, ServerConfig, DatabaseConfig, LogConfig, OAuthConfig, OAuthProvider structs
Use exact TOML field names: [server] port, bind, external_url; [database] path; [log] level; [[oauth.providers]] name, client_id, client_secret, authorize_url, token_url, userinfo_url
Set struct defaults: Port=8080, Bind="0.0.0.0", Path="./data/af-hub.db", Level="info"

subtasks.title: Implement loadConfig using BurntSushi/toml with missing-file, unknown-field, and invalid-level handling
details: Use toml.DecodeFile; on os.IsNotExist error → apply all defaults silently
On TOML parse error → logrus.Fatalf with parse failure reason + os.Exit(1)
Use md.Undecoded() to detect unrecognized fields; emit logrus.Warn per field with name/path
Use logrus.ParseLevel for log.level; on error emit warn with invalid_value field and default to info
Return (*Config, configDir string) where configDir is the directory of the resolved config path

subtasks.title: Implement CLI flag parsing for --config and --reset-admin-token
details: Use standard library flag package in cmd/af-hub/main.go
Define --config string flag (default: ./config.toml) and --reset-admin-token bool flag
Flags are parsed first before any other initialization (step 1 of startup sequence)
Both flags are independent; combining them is fully supported

checks: Config unit tests pass: go test ./internal/config/... -count=1 -v
TS-01-1 through TS-01-5 and TS-01-E1 pass
No linter errors: go vet ./internal/config/...
go mod tidy confirms github.com/BurntSushi/toml is in go.mod

subtasks.title: Implement initLogging function using logrus with JSONFormatter and stdout
details: Create internal/logging/logging.go
Call logrus.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})
Call logrus.SetOutput(os.Stdout)
Call logrus.SetLevel(parsedLevel) where parsedLevel comes from config (already validated)
Ensure go.mod pins github.com/sirupsen/logrus v1.4.0 minimum
No stderr or file output — stdout only

subtasks.title: Implement request logger middleware with request ID generation and propagation
details: Create internal/middleware/request_logger.go
On each request: check X-Request-ID header — validate non-empty, printable ASCII (0x20-0x7E), ≤128 chars; discard invalid; generate uuid.New().String() if absent or invalid
Attach request_id to Echo context via c.Set(string(AuthContextKey), ...) — use a separate ContextKey for request ID
Set X-Request-ID response header before returning
After handler returns: log with logrus.WithFields{method, path, status, duration_ms, request_id}

checks: Logging and request ID tests pass: go test ./internal/logging/... ./internal/middleware/... -run 'TestLog|TestRequestID' -count=1 -v
TS-01-17 through TS-01-22 and TS-01-E9 pass
go mod tidy confirms logrus v1.4.0 pin in go.mod
No linter errors: go vet ./internal/logging/... ./internal/middleware/...

subtasks.title: Implement database open with parent directory creation and PRAGMA application
details: Create internal/db/db.go; import _ "modernc.org/sqlite"
os.MkdirAll(parentDir, 0755) before sql.Open; on error → logrus.Fatalf + os.Exit(1)
sql.Open("sqlite", dbPath) then db.Ping(); on error → logrus.Fatalf + os.Exit(1)
Apply pragmas in order: PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL, PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON, PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000
On any PRAGMA exec error → logrus.Fatalf identifying the specific PRAGMA name + os.Exit(1)

subtasks.title: Implement schema creation: all 7 tables and 5 indexes via CREATE TABLE/INDEX IF NOT EXISTS
details: Execute DDL for all tables: users, admin_tokens, api_keys, teams, team_members, workspaces, workspace_tokens
Execute DDL for all indexes: idx_api_keys_key_id, idx_workspace_tokens_token_id, idx_users_provider, idx_workspaces_slug, idx_teams_slug
All statements use IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency
No SetMaxOpenConns or SetMaxIdleConns overrides; rely on busy_timeout pragma
Return *sql.DB to caller

subtasks.title: Implement health probe handlers: /healthz and /readyz with atomic failure counter
details: Create internal/handler/health.go
healthzHandler: return c.JSON(200, map[string]string{"status": "ok"})
readyzHandler: ctx with 2s timeout; db.QueryRowContext(ctx, "SELECT 1"); on success: check counter > 0 → log info recovery + atomic.StoreInt64(&counter, 0); return 200 ready
On failure: atomic.AddInt64(&counter, 1); if counter==1 → error log; else → debug log; return 503 {"status": "not ready"}
package-level var readyzFailureCounter int64 accessed exclusively via sync/atomic

checks: DB initialization tests pass: go test ./internal/db/... -count=1 -v
Health probe tests pass: go test ./internal/handler/... -run 'TestHealth' -count=1 -v
TS-01-8 through TS-01-16 and TS-01-E4 through TS-01-E8 pass
No linter errors: go vet ./internal/db/... ./internal/handler/...

task_groups.title: Implement admin bootstrap, token validation, and rotation

subtasks.title: Implement token generation helpers and admin_token file write
details: Create internal/admin/bootstrap.go
generateAdminToken(): use crypto/rand to generate 32 random bytes; hex.EncodeToString → 64 hex chars; prepend af_admin_
hashAdminTokenSuffix(suffix string) string: return hex.EncodeToString(sha256.Sum256([]byte(suffix)))
writeAdminTokenFile(path, token string): os.WriteFile with perm 0600; if file exists → warn log with path; if write fails → logrus.Fatalf + os.Exit(1)
Log absolute path of written file at warn level after successful write

subtasks.title: Implement first-boot admin bootstrap flow
details: Count rows in admin_tokens; if 0 → first boot path
If AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env var is set → log info notice and ignore it
Insert admin user row: id=uuid.New(), username=admin, email=admin@localhost, provider=local, provider_id=admin, status=active
Generate token, compute hash, insert into admin_tokens, write plaintext to configDir/admin_token
On subsequent boot: check if admin user row missing → continue normally (admin token auth does no user lookup)

subtasks.title: Implement subsequent-boot token validation and --reset-admin-token rotation
details: Subsequent boot: read AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env; if absent → logrus.Fatalf + os.Exit(1)
Compute SHA-256 of env var value; compare to stored hash; mismatch → logrus.Fatalf + os.Exit(1)
Check if admin_token file exists at configDir/admin_token: if present → warn log with path and msg; if absent → silence
--reset-admin-token: skip validation entirely; delete existing admin_tokens row; generate new token; insert new hash; write file; log path; continue startup
--reset-admin-token with empty admin_tokens: identical to first boot (no special-casing)

checks: Admin bootstrap tests pass: go test ./internal/admin/... -count=1 -v
TS-01-26 through TS-01-34 and TS-01-E11 through TS-01-E14 pass
Property test TS-01-P1 and TS-01-P9 pass: go test ./internal/admin/... -run 'TestProp' -count=1 -v
No linter errors: go vet ./internal/admin/...

subtasks.title: Define AuthContext, ContextKey types and token parsing helpers
details: Create internal/middleware/auth.go
Define ContextKey string type, AuthContextKey ContextKey = "auth_context"
Define CredentialType string type with constants: CredentialTypeAdmin, CredentialTypeAPIKey, CredentialTypeWorkspaceToken
Define AuthContext struct with CredentialType, UserID, WorkspaceID, IsAdmin fields
Implement token parsing: detect prefix (af_admin_, af_wt_, af_); structural validation before any DB call; return 401 immediately on structural failure

subtasks.title: Implement admin token verification in auth middleware
details: Strip af_admin_ prefix; validate suffix is exactly 64 hex chars
Compute sha256 of suffix; query admin_tokens for stored hash
Compare using subtle.ConstantTimeCompare; mismatch → HTTP 401
On match: set AuthContext{CredentialType=admin, IsAdmin=true, UserID="", WorkspaceID=""}; no users table lookup; blocked-user check bypassed entirely
Store in Echo context: c.Set(string(AuthContextKey), &authCtx)

subtasks.title: Implement user API key and workspace token verification in auth middleware
details: API key: split on _ yielding 3 parts; validate key_id (8 alphanumeric), secret (32 alphanumeric); query api_keys by key_id; sha256(secret) vs secret_hash via subtle.ConstantTimeCompare
Check expires_at (exclusive UTC: expires_at < now → 401) and revoked_at (IS NOT NULL → 401); no distinction between expired/revoked in response
Workspace token: strip af_wt_ prefix; split remainder on _ yielding 2 parts; validate token_id (8 alphanumeric), secret (32 alphanumeric); query workspace_tokens; same hash/expiry/revocation checks
After hash+state validation: query users table for status; if status=blocked → HTTP 403
On success: set AuthContext with correct CredentialType, UserID from users row, WorkspaceID (workspace token only)

subtasks.title: Implement DB write contention error handling in auth middleware
details: Detect SQLite busy/locked error from driver (check error string for SQLITE_BUSY or use error code)
Return HTTP 503 with {"error": {"code": 503, "message": "service temporarily unavailable"}} for write contention
Server remains running; no shutdown triggered
Same 503 envelope applies for write contention during normal handler DB writes (not just auth)

checks: Auth middleware tests pass: go test ./internal/middleware/... -run 'TestAuth' -count=1 -v -race
TS-01-45 through TS-01-52 and TS-01-E15 through TS-01-E19 pass
Property tests TS-01-P2 through TS-01-P5 pass: go test ./internal/middleware/... -run 'TestProp' -count=1 -v
No linter errors: go vet ./internal/middleware/...

task_groups.title: Implement Echo route groups, middleware stack, custom error handler, and graceful shutdown

subtasks.title: Implement custom error handler for non-health-probe routes
details: Create internal/handler/error.go
customErrorHandler(err error, c echo.Context): type-switch on *echo.HTTPError vs generic error
For *echo.HTTPError: use Code and Message.(string) for envelope; for other: HTTP 500 internal server error
Assign to e.HTTPErrorHandler; skip envelope for /healthz and /readyz paths
Panic recovery via Echo's Recover middleware surfaces through this handler → HTTP 500 envelope

subtasks.title: Implement Echo route group structure and middleware registration
details: Create internal/server/server.go
e.Use(middleware.Recover(), bodySizeLimitMiddleware(1<<20), requestLoggerMiddleware())
Register GET and HEAD /healthz and /readyz on root Echo instance (no group)
authGroup := e.Group("/api/v1/auth") — no auth middleware
protectedGroup := e.Group("/api/v1", authMiddleware(db)) — auth middleware applied
e.HTTPErrorHandler = customErrorHandler

subtasks.title: Implement graceful shutdown handler with 15-second drain timeout
details: In main.go: signal.NotifyContext or signal.Notify for os.Interrupt and syscall.SIGTERM
On signal: ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 15*time.Second); defer cancel()
Call e.Shutdown(ctx); if err == context.DeadlineExceeded → logrus.Warn fixed message; else → logrus.Info server shutdown complete
Delegates in-flight tracking entirely to net/http; no separate counter

checks: Route group and error handler tests pass: go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/handler/... -count=1 -v
TS-01-35 through TS-01-44 pass
Graceful shutdown tests pass: go test ./internal/server/... -run 'TestShutdown' -count=1 -v
No linter errors: go vet ./internal/server/... ./internal/handler/...

task_groups.title: Implement main.go startup sequence and binary wiring

subtasks.title: Implement main.go with strict startup sequence steps 1-9
details: Create cmd/af-hub/main.go
Step 1: flag.Parse() for --config and --reset-admin-token
Step 2: loadConfig(configPath) → cfg, configDir
Step 3: initLogging(cfg.Log.Level)
Step 4: db = initDatabase(cfg.Database.Path)
Step 5: runAdminBootstrap(db, resetFlag, configDir) or validateAdminToken(db, configDir)
Step 6: e = echo.New(); registerRoutes(e, db); e.HTTPErrorHandler = customErrorHandler
Step 7: logrus.WithFields{bind, port, db_path, log_level}.Info("server starting")
Step 8: go e.Start(addr) in goroutine
Step 9: arm signal handler; block until signal; call e.Shutdown with 15s timeout

subtasks.title: Configure go.mod with all required dependencies and version pins
details: Ensure go.mod has: require github.com/sirupsen/logrus v1.4.0 (minimum pin)
Add github.com/labstack/echo/v4, github.com/BurntSushi/toml, modernc.org/sqlite, github.com/google/uuid
Run go mod tidy and go mod verify to confirm no conflicts
Create Makefile with `make build` target: go build -o bin/af-hub ./cmd/af-hub/

subtasks.title: Implement body-size limit middleware (1 MB cap) with 413 error handling
details: Use echo/middleware.BodyLimit("1M") or implement custom middleware using http.MaxBytesReader
Ensure 413 is wrapped through the custom error handler producing the standard envelope
No-op for GET/HEAD requests (no body to read); middleware wraps response writer in all cases but triggers only on body read

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration and smoke tests

subtasks.title: Trace all execution paths and verify no stubs remain
details: Search codebase for stub markers: grep -r 'panic("not implemented")' ./; grep -r 'TODO' ./ | grep -v '_test.go'
Trace PATH-1 (first boot): main → loadConfig → initLogging → initDatabase → runAdminBootstrap → registerRoutes → Start
Trace PATH-2 (subsequent boot): main → validateAdminToken → registerRoutes → Start
Trace PATH-3 (reset): main → --reset-admin-token flag → generateAndStoreNewToken → registerRoutes → Start
Trace PATH-4 (admin token auth): request → Recover → BodyLimit → RequestLogger → authMiddleware → handler → response

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation across all module boundaries
details: Confirm loadConfig returns (*Config, configDir) and main.go uses both values
Confirm initDatabase returns *sql.DB and db is passed to authMiddleware and handler constructors
Confirm AuthContext is stored in Echo context and retrievable by downstream handlers
Confirm readyz failure counter is the package-level atomic var (not a local copy)
Confirm e.Shutdown return value is inspected to distinguish clean drain vs timeout

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests against the real binary
details: Build binary: make build
SMOKE-1: run bin/af-hub in clean temp dir; verify DB, tables, admin_token file mode 0600, /healthz 200, /readyz 200

SMOKE-8: POST with 2MB body; verify 413 with envelope; status=413 in log

subtasks.title: Verify cross-spec entry points are exported and accessible
details: Confirm authGroup (*echo.Group at /api/v1/auth) and protectedGroup (*echo.Group at /api/v1) are exported or returned from server constructor for downstream specs (2-5) to register routes
Verify /api/v1/auth/* paths do not receive 401 from auth middleware (structural exclusion, not string matching)
Verify /api/v1/* paths receive 401 without auth header
Confirm AuthContextKey, AuthContext, CredentialType types are exported from internal/middleware package for use by downstream specs

checks: All smoke tests pass: go test ./internal/integration/... -run 'TestSmoke' -count=1 -v -timeout=120s
No stub markers found: grep -r 'panic("not implemented")' ./internal/ returns empty
No TODO stubs in production code: grep -r 'TODO' ./internal/ | grep -v '_test.go' returns empty or only documented deferred items
Full test suite passes with race detector: go test ./... -count=1 -race -timeout=120s
Binary builds and starts cleanly in a fresh temp directory: make build && (cd /tmp/af-hub-test && $OLDPWD/bin/af-hub &) && sleep 1 && curl -sf http://localhost:8080/healthz
No linter errors in any package: go vet ./...
